record.field: Cloudreve is a self-hosted file management and sharing platform that turns local disks and multiple cloud providers into a single, unified cloud drive.

Cloudreve is a self-hosted file management and sharing platform that turns local disks and multiple cloud providers into a single, unified cloud drive.

It builds a virtual file system on top of a database, decoupling what users see from where bytes physically live, so files can span local storage and many object stores at once. Aimed at individuals, teams, and providers who want a private alternative to commercial cloud storage, it stands out for its broad storage-backend support, direct client-to-storage transfers, and built-in clustering for scaling out.

Key features
  • Many storage backends: local disk, remote nodes, S3-compatible, OneDrive, Aliyun OSS, Tencent COS, Huawei OBS, Qiniu, and more
  • Virtual file system abstracting physical storage, with users, groups, and per-policy storage
  • Direct client-side uploads/downloads to storage without relaying through the server
  • WebDAV access plus share links with expiration, resumable drag-and-drop uploads
  • Online preview for video, images, audio, ePub, and Office documents; aria2/qBittorrent offline downloads
  • Primary-replica clustering and security options such as FIDO2 keys and two-factor authentication
Best for

Individuals and teams building a private cloud drive, and service providers offering multi-tenant file storage who need to combine local and multiple cloud backends behind one interface.

services: cloudreve: image: cloudreve/cloudreve:4.10.1 depends_on: postgresql: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_started restart: unless-stopped environment: - CR_CONF_Database.Type=postgres - CR_CONF_Database.Host=postgresql - CR_CONF_Database.User={{ postgres_user }} - CR_CONF_Database.Password={{ postgres_password }} - CR_CONF_Database.Name={{ postgres_db }} - CR_CONF_Database.Port=5432 - CR_CONF_Redis.Server=redis:6379 volumes: - ./cloudreve_data:/cloudreve/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:5212' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network postgresql: image: postgres:17 restart: unless-stopped environment: - POSTGRES_USER={{ postgres_user }} - POSTGRES_PASSWORD={{ postgres_password }} - POSTGRES_DB={{ postgres_db }} volumes: - ./postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U {{ postgres_user }} -d {{ postgres_db }} interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5 start_period: 30s container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_postgresql' networks: - internal redis: image: redis:8.4.0 restart: unless-stopped volumes: - ./redis_data:/data container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_redis' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T postgresql sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d postgresql for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T postgresql sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T postgresql sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T postgresql sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
